title: DESIGN.md — Bass-Ackwards Hierarchical Structural Analysis (R package)
text: scoring scheme scores linear map standardized observed variables, S = Z W, -level score correlations exactly: scores need materialized — R weight matrices. Waller (2007) derived orthogonal components, identity holds linear W, includes regression (Thurstone, W = R^{-1} Λ), Bartlett, tenBerge scores. standard PCA EFA/ESEM (regression-scored) paths algebra-eligible. Standardization trap. Component/factor scores unit-variance general (Bartlett oblique scores especially). Always divide real score SDs sqrt(diag(W'RW)). Assuming unit variance silently corrupts correlations.
code: cor(S_a, S_b) = D_a^{-1/2} (W_a' R W_b) D_b^{-1/2}            where  R   = input correlation matrix (pearson or polychoric)                   D_x = diag(W_x' R W_x)   # score variances; NOT assumed 1

title: DESIGN.md — Bass-Ackwards Hierarchical Structural Analysis (R package)
text: Two situations force scores route: 1. Nonlinear scoring — EAP/MAP/ML scores categorical/ordinal ESEM. (ordinal ESEM instead uses regression scores polychoric loadings, stays linear → algebra polychoric R.) 2. User wants empirical, sample-realized correlations (including FIML/missing-data realities) rather model-implied quantity. missingness differ; document one “edge” represents (default: model-consistent / algebra eligible).
code: compute_edges(levels, R,               edge_method = c("auto", "algebra", "scores"),               pairs  = c("adjacent", "all"),   # "all" for Forbes extension               data   = NULL,               align  = TRUE,               use    = "pairwise") {    for (each pair (a, b) in chosen pairs) {      algebra_ok <- levels[[a]]$scoring$linear &&                   levels[[b]]$scoring$linear && !is.null(R)      if (edge_method == "algebra" || (edge_method == "auto" && algebra_ok)) {       Wa <- levels[[a]]$scoring$weights       Wb <- levels[[b]]$scoring$weights       C  <- crossprod(Wa, R %*% Wb)                       # W_a' R W_b       sa <- sqrt(diag(crossprod(Wa, R %*% Wa)))           # score SDs       sb <- sqrt(diag(crossprod(Wb, R %*% Wb)))       E  <- sweep(sweep(C, 1, sa, "/"), 2, sb, "/")       # standardize     } else {       stopifnot(!is.null(data))                           # nonlinear/EAP or edge_method="scores"       Sa <- score(levels[[a]], data); Sb <- score(levels[[b]], data)       E  <- cor(Sa, Sb, use = use)     }      if (align) E <- align_signs(E, lineage)               # see §7     edges[[pair]] <- E   }   # return: list of (k_a x k_b) matrices  +  tidy edge tibble (from, to, r, is_primary, above_cut) }

title: DESIGN.md — Bass-Ackwards Hierarchical Structural Analysis (R package)
text: IDs: models numbered factor count (m1…mK); factors within model m{k}f{j}. Deterministic within-level order = primary-parent (recursive), variance tiebreak. Sort factors within level (primary parent’s position level , descending variance). single top factor ties fall back variance. places child parent splits read cleanly; f{j} ID order follows layout order tables plots agree. Limitation: ordering reduces can’t eliminate crossings (child strong secondary parent elsewhere still throws crossing edge); layout’s barycenter step (§11) refines x-position continuously beyond discrete order. Lineage lives edges/lineage, never ID. child can multiple strong parents (point method), don’t encode parentage m{k}f{j} — breaks structure isn’t clean tree. Matching: assign factor primary parent via greedy per-column argmax |r| (.e. apply(abs(E), 2, .max)). Multiple children sharing parent normal expected — adjacent levels always n_b = n_a + 1 (level k k factors vs k−1 ), pigeonhole least two children share parent. bijection (LSAP/Hungarian) therefore ill-posed removed. Tucker’s φ serves supplementary congruence criterion redundancy_phi filtering (Forbes extension, §14.19) used matching . Sign alignment — anchor primary parent, “positive.” Sign one DoF per factor; make every cross-level correlation positive. Rule: anchor m1f1 defined orientation (positive sum loadings, substantive marker item), orient factor correlation primary parent positive, propagating top-. Document non-primary edges may legitimately negative. Implementation note (M35): “propagating top-” means child’s flip chosen parent’s already-aligned sign, parent’s raw orientation — otherwise flipped parent leaves primary edge displaying negative. every primary-parent edge non-negative; secondary edges may red.

Skipping real compute saving, output filtering: pa_pc/pa_fa share one fa.parallel(fa = "") call (neither run) map/vss share one vss() call, e.g. criteria = "map" avoids PA simulation entirely; vss toggles VSS-1+VSS-2 unit. Non-requested k_* fields NA; criteria data frame keeps stable schema (NA-filled non-run columns); object records criteria_requested. print()/autoplot() render requested criteria consensus range computed requested criteria . Empirical Kaiser Criterion (EKC) EGA (EGAnet) scope — additional deps without sufficient incremental benefit five criteria . Note: suggest_k() accepts cor = "pearson" (default) "spearman" ; "polychoric" supported (PA VSS polychoric eigen-decomposition path). Users analysing ordinal data run suggest_k() Pearson matrix switch cor = "polychoric" ackwards(). Report k maximum depth; users often deliberately set level two past consensus watch factors fragment. Note overextraction/non-replicability caution (Forbes 2023). Add seed argument reproducibility CD step — psych::fa.parallel() respond reliably set.seed(), PA simulation results vary across calls regardless seed. See vignette("ackwards-suggest-k") narrative/educational treatment five criteria including pros/cons, bias direction, engine--criterion pairing (M14).

title: DESIGN.md — Bass-Ackwards Hierarchical Structural Analysis (R package)
text: Split layout (light, core) rendering (optional, Suggests). keeps dependency footprint sane lets users plot layout however like. Top-pass – determines left--right order level. factor’s rank |r|-weighted mean parents’ ranks, grouping siblings share parent minimising crossings. Bottom-pass – assigns actual x coordinates. deepest level (level k) evenly spaced; every upper-level factor placed simple mean x primary children: factor one primary child lands directly ; factor multiple primary children lands exactly halfway . Falls back |r|-weighted mean children factors primary children. Spreading enforces min_sep adjacent nodes needed. level-1 node shifted x = 0 passes. produces “boxes aligned primary-child structure” look owner wants. autoplot.ackwards() / plot() (Suggests: ggplot2). Renders layered diagram ba_layout() + edge tibble. Edge encodings user-assignable always legended (M35): sign_by chooses channel sign (color default; also linetype, , none) magnitude_by chooses channel |r| (linewidth default, none). aesthetic mapped without matching legend. (pre-M35 strong/weak cut_strong linetype split retired — double-encoded magnitude legend-less.) Nodes labeled m{k}f{j}, optionally substantive labels top-loading items. direction toggles vertical (default, level 1 top) vs. horizontal (level 1 left) orientation. Forbes extension rendering (complex — phase ). Keep layout; annotate rather re-layout: fade/strike pruned (redundant/artifact) nodes, allow skip-level edges (longer curves) since extension correlates levels, just adjacent. title: DESIGN.md — Bass-Ackwards Hierarchical Structural Analysis (R package)
text: Resolved (design round): 1. Rotation → varimax (orthogonal) across engines, hardcoded internal constant since M13; oblique rotation scope (resolved 2026-06 — confounds cross-level signal method’s whole point). rotation removed user argument M13. 2. Correlation basis → pearson default, polychoric opt-, ordinal-detection cli warning (silent switching). 3. Within-level order → primary-parent (recursive), variance tiebreak; f{j} IDs follow . 4. Ordinal ESEM scoring → tenBerge polychoric (linear → algebra) default; EAP opt-. Default Likert path need data edge time. 5. Package name → ackwards (verify via available::available("ackwards")). Resolved build (M1–M3): 6. Ordinal-detection heuristic: ≤ 7 distinct integer values triggers warning. 7. CF kappa: κ = 1/p (varimax-equivalent; Crawford & Ferguson 1970). kappa argument accepted stored never wired engine — engines hardcoded cfT → "varimax". Removed entirely M13: CF(κ=1/p) ≡ varimax; literature never varies kappa; exposing implied quartimax/equamax reasonable alternatives (method). 8. cor = "spearman" added alongside "pearson" non-polychoric rank-based option. 9. fm argument exposed EFA engine ("minres" default, "ml", "pa"). 10. cut_show argument exposed (default 0.3) control edges flagged above_cut. Resolved M4: 11. ESEM engine → lavaan::efa() (EFA--SEM, full ESEM structural paths). Gives per-level fit indices + rotation-aware SEs + WLSMV ordinal estimation. require full ESEM block syntax; bass-ackwards levels independent EFA solutions. 12. ESEM scoring → self-compute tenBerge weights lavaan’s estimated loadings Λ latent correlation matrix R via existing .tenBerge_weights(R, Λ). lavPredict() used default path (lacks tenBerge); eventual hook EAP opt-. 13. EAP scoring → scope (declined M28). EAP’s shrinkage attenuates cross-level correlations — primary signal bass-ackwards measures — making theoretically inferior tenBerge method. EAP request still returns cli_abort("yet implemented"). scores-route seam compute_edges() preserved, implementing EAP planned. 14. cor = "polychoric" → general basis engines (ESEM-). PCA/EFA compute R via psych::polychoric() ackwards() feed engine usual. ESEM uses lavaan’s latent correlation matrix R edges (mix psych-polychoric R lavaan-WLSMV loadings). 15. ESEM ordinal estimator → WLSMV default; ULSMV documented option. 16. loadings_se → added §4 level contract (p×k matrix, NULL PCA/EFA). Completed M4 (addition items 11–16 ): - NPD polychoric matrices: warn + smooth via psych::cor.smooth() (implemented). - Convergence truncation tested ESEM engine (k=4+ p=6 triggers lavaan error → warn + truncate → object builds k_eff=3). - Algebra-vs-scores cross-check documented Pearson/continuous paths ; polychoric ESEM edges (algebra uses lavaan polychoric R; scores route uses Pearson standardization) diverge design excluded oracle. Resolved M5 (Forbes extension): 17. API → two orthogonal args: pairs = c("adjacent","") prune = c("none","redundant","artefact") (char vector; default "none"). prune != "none" auto-upgrades pairs "" loud cli_inform() (chains need -levels edges assess). Superseded M34 (item 27 ): pruning now standalone prune() verb, ackwards() argument, pairs longer auto-upgrades. 18. Prune action → flag-, never remove. Adds pruned/prune_reason annotation columns edge/node tidy structures; object retains levels (preserves Invariant 5 algebra-vs-scores oracle). Pruning interpretive relabeling, re-estimation — say print/docs (extends §2 honesty caveat). 19. Redundancy → faithful Forbes (2023): score-correlation chains |r| ≥ .9 (default, tunable), retention rule = keep bottom node chain reaches level k (-specific, best-defined), else keep topmost node (broadest manifestation). Tucker’s φ (> .95, Lorenzo-Seva & ten Berge 2006) computed aligned loadings optional conjunctive criterion. φ formula Σaᵢbᵢ / sqrt(Σaᵢ² · Σbᵢ²); base R, new dependency. 20. Additive enrichments paper (default output still matches Forbes’s examples): always report r φ every redundancy candidate (report-first, flag-second — borderline cases like paper’s .89/.93 alcohol component stay visible); report endpoint r (direct, -levels edges) alongside chain flag disagree (correlation non-transitive: clean adjacent chain neither implies implied endpoint identity — chain answers “perpetuates every level,” endpoint r answers “construct”). 21. Artifact → never auto-flagged. prune(x, "artifact") surfaces φ inspection; removal documented researcher judgment (Forbes explicit introduces researcher DoF / confirmation bias; cf. Wicherts et al. 2016). Resolved M32 (API-shape & naming; owner-reviewed, equivalent guidance elsewhere document): 22. tidy(x, = "fit") long-format key column → statistic, replacing index (read row position; actually held fit-index names EFA/ESEM eigenvalue positions PCA). Wide format (format = "wide") unaffected — column names still come values key. 23. k_max naming collision (ackwards() = extraction depth vs. suggest_k() = max factors/components evaluated) → keep shared name functions. ’re genuinely dial applied different workflow stages (suggest_k(k_max = ...) → ackwards(k_max = ...)); renaming one create vocabulary drift without removing real ambiguity. Resolved instead via roxygen: function’s @param k_max states meaning cross-references ’s. 24. tidy(= "fit") cutoff flags → removed. cutoffs = TRUE argument meets/{statistic}_meets columns produced (.flag_fit()) dropped; pass/fail boolean quietly endorsed Hu & Bentler (1999) thresholds package elsewhere (§9, section) treats conventional contested — continuing M28/M31 output-honesty trajectory. .fit_cutoffs() retained internal helper: autoplot(= "fit") still draws threshold reference lines summary() still annotates inline check/cross mark: visual guides, returned pass/fail column user mistake computed judgment. 25. Variance scale → proportion, 0-1 (tidy(= "variance") columns renamed variance_pct/cumulative_pct → proportion/cumulative, values divided 100). aligns tidy() engine’s internal variance slot (already 0-1; see print.ackwards, reads directly) broom/psych convention (psych reports “Proportion Var” 0-1). Percent formatting moves display layer (print(), summary(), vignette gt tables) rather living tidy data. 26. M31-deferred: effective ESEM estimator recorded $meta. M31 explicitly deferred (“better bundled M32’s meta/column decisions bolted ”): x$meta$estimator now stores effective estimator auto-selection ("ML"/"MLR"/"WLSMV"/"ULSMV"; NA PCA/EFA). summary() gains one-line footnote naming lavaan’s scaled-variant reporting (§14.M31 point 2/Post-review) whenever effective estimator "WLSMV"/"ULSMV"/"MLR" — shown "ML", scaled variant. five M32 changes breaking deprecation path (pre-CRAN, users; consistent M34 pruning-verb precedent clean moves compatibility shims). Resolved M34 (pruning verb; owner-reviewed, breaking, deprecation path — pre-CRAN, users): 27. Pruning extracted standalone, pipeable S3 generic prune() (prune.ackwards), ackwards() argument. five prune-related args (prune → renamed rules, redundancy_r, redundancy_phi, min_items, orphan_r) leave ackwards() entirely: ackwards(...) |> prune(...). Rationale: extraction expensive, deterministic step; pruning cheap interpretive. Separating lets researcher re-prune new thresholds without re-extracting (re-running ESEM per level expensive path M26 optimized). prune() generic (UseMethod), plain function, coexists prune generics already defined recursive-partitioning packages (e.g. rpart::prune) regardless package load order. Returns ackwards object $prune populated (replacing prior pruning) — new class, print/summary/tidy/glance/augment/ autoplot work unchanged. 28. Edges redundancy chains artifact φ recomputed fresh inside prune() via compute_edges(pairs = "") object’s stored levels/r (Invariant 3), never written back x$edges (Invariant 1: one edge path). ackwards()‘s pairs auto-upgrade "" pruning requested (M5 item 17) removed along — pairs now pure display/storage setting ackwards(), decoupled pruning; prune() works correctly regardless fit-time pairs value. 29. Manual pruning: prune(x, rules = "none", manual = c("m4f3", "m4f4")) flags user-named nodes directly (standalone, auto rule needed), unions onto auto rule’s flags (prune(x, "redundant", manual = c(...))). Unknown labels error. overlap auto rule manual, auto rule’s prune_reason wins (informative); otherwise-unflagged manual nodes get prune_reason = "manual". 30. Naming: canonical rule name "artifact" (US spelling, owner’s preference), "artefact" accepted alias (nod Commonwealth spelling Forbes’ usage) normalized internally "artifact". Existing code passing "artefact" keeps working. "tucker" considered rejected alias/rename: mode surfaces Tucker’s φ (also computes few_items/orphan/split_merge structural signals), naming statistic mislabel umbrella — "artifact" names mode . 31. ackwards() explicitly rejects five removed args passed via ... (rather silently absorbing ) pointer prune() — silent absorption masked-argument footgun, clean break intended (Invariant 6: loud, silent). Known limitations / deferred future milestones: - factor_cor ESEM engine permuted variance-sort ord vector. Safe permanently: orthogonal rotation supported (factor_cor = ; permutation ), oblique rotation scope (§9, §14.1). guard comment engine_esem.R documents required decision ever reversed. - Algebra-vs-scores cross-check cover cor = "polychoric" paths (see ). - cor = "spearman" + engine = "esem" semantically inconsistent (lavaan fits Pearson ML raw data edges use Spearman R); warning now emitted (M10). - ESEM engine detect warn improper/Heywood solutions — resolved M10: engine now inspects lavaan::lavInspect(fit, "theta") warns (diag(theta) <= 0) (parity EFA engine, Invariant 7). - ESEM ML/MLR missing = "pairwise": lavaan uses listwise deletion model fit edges computed separately-computed pairwise stats::cor() — minor inconsistency (fit statistics complete-case N, edges full pairwise N). Documented $meta$missing; per-call advisory warning fires whenever NAs detected. Use missing = "listwise" "fiml" resolve. (Added M16; §9 missing row cross-references .) - Forbes-extension improvements deferred past M5 (published method weaker spots worth strengthening later; M5 ships faithful method + §14.20 reporting enrichments): - Structural artefact signals. Done M25 (Wave 2). prune = "artefact" now populates x$prune$structural per-factor few_items / orphan / split_merge signals (flag/report ; auto-pruning). Args: min_items = 3L, orphan_r = 0.5. split_merge TRUE factor’s primary items came multiple different primary factors preceding level. CLI print()/summary() report flagged count. - Factor-score-indeterminacy caveat EFA/ESEM redundancy. Done M25 (Wave 3). redundancy_phi = NULL now auto-resolves: PCA → φ filter; EFA/ESEM → 0.95. NA explicit opt-. Announces via cli auto-applied (Invariant 6). See §9 defaults table. - Selection bias “strongest” edge. Plotting max correlation across many -levels pairs capitalizes chance (85 → 1,320 correlations levels grow). Add bootstrap CIs / SEs edges (reuse loadings_se infrastructure) strongest-edge claim inferentially honest. Deferred; warrants milestone (perf-heavy: resample re-runs full extraction every level; needs future parallelisation + print/plot/vignette integration).

title: Choosing an Engine: PCA, EFA, and ESEM
text: PCA extracts principal components — linear combinations observed variables capture maximum variance, including measurement error. Every item modeled communality 1.0: components account 100% item’s variance. true latent variable model; data reduction method. bass-ackwards context, PCA natural default. fast, always converges, produces eigenvalues can guide choice k. Waller (2007) showed -level algebra (W'RW) holds exactly components, making edges algebraically exact rather approximated materialized scores. “fit” PCA just eigenvalue component — amount variance captures. chi-square tests, RMSEA, model rejection.
code: x_pca <- ackwards(bfi, k_max = 3, cor = "polychoric") x_pca #>  #> ── Bass-Ackwards Analysis (ackwards) ─────────────────────────────────────────── #> Engine: pca #> Rotation: varimax #> Basis: polychoric #> n: 875 #> k (max): 3 #>  #> ── Levels ── #>  #> ✔ k = 1: 1 factor, 23.2% variance #> ✔ k = 2: 2 factors, 35.5% variance #> ✔ k = 3: 3 factors, 44.6% variance #>  #> ── Edges ── #>  #> 5 of 8 edges have |r| ≥ 0.3 #> ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> Note: This is a series of linked solutions, not a fitted hierarchical model. #> Cross-level edges are descriptive score correlations. Per-level fit indices #> (EFA/ESEM) describe how well a k-factor model fits the items at that level -- #> they do not validate the edges or the hierarchy itself. tidy(x_pca, what = "fit") #>   level       statistic    value #> 1     1 eigenvalue.m1f1 5.802803 #> 2     2 eigenvalue.m2f1 5.802803 #> 3     2 eigenvalue.m2f2 3.067627 #> 4     3 eigenvalue.m3f1 5.802803 #> 5     3 eigenvalue.m3f2 3.067627 #> 6     3 eigenvalue.m3f3 2.275419

title: Choosing an Engine: PCA, EFA, and ESEM
text: ackwards() accepts missing argument three options. "pairwise" (default). what: Why Pearson underestimates for ordinal items

title: Choosing k: How Many Factors?
text: . Computes PC eigenvalues observed correlation matrix, simulates large number random correlation matrices dimensions. Retains components whose observed eigenvalues exceed 95th percentile simulated distribution. Typical behavior. Tends overextract — often recommends components replicate independent samples, particularly items moderately correlated (usually personality clinical research). Treat recommendation upper bound. useful. Best paired engine = "pca" ackwards(), since operate PC eigenvalue basis. also provides fast, stable baseline across wide range data types. title: Customizing the Hierarchy Diagram
text: default diagram reference:  Factors labeled m{k}f{j} (level k, factor j). Two edge aesthetics carry -level correlations, one comes legend: arrow thickness encodes magnitude |r|, edge color encodes direction (blue = positive, red–orange = negative). Level labels left count factors per level. Primary-parent edges always positive sign alignment; red (negative) edge therefore genuine secondary relationship, artifact.
code: library(ackwards)  bfi <- na.omit(bfi25) x <- ackwards(bfi, k_max = 5, cor = "polychoric") autoplot(x)

title: Customizing the Hierarchy Diagram

text: sign_by = "color" (default) uses color_pos/color_neg. "linetype" draws positive edges solid negative edges dashed, freeing color single-hue figure. title: Bass-Ackwards Hierarchical Structural Analysis
text: Bass-ackwards hierarchical structural analysis R ackwards implements Goldberg’s (2006) bass-ackwards method modern extensions mapping hierarchical structure multivariate data. core insight simple: instead asking many factors best describe data, ask solutions different levels resolution related one another. 1-factor solution captures broadest shared variance; 5-factor solution captures narrower, specific dimensions. Bass-ackwards analysis traces broad factors split narrow ones — narrow factors redundant re-combinations broader ones. package supports three extraction engines (PCA, EFA, ESEM), polychoric correlations ordinal data, Forbes’s (2023) extension reveals skip-level connections flags redundant artifactual factors.

title: Bass-Ackwards Hierarchical Structural Analysis
text: ackwards() fits factor models every level 1 k_max computes -level factor-score correlations define hierarchy.
code: x <- ackwards(bfi, k_max = 5, cor = "polychoric") x #>  #> ── Bass-Ackwards Analysis (ackwards) ─────────────────────────────────────────── #> Engine: pca #> Rotation: varimax #> Basis: polychoric #> n: 875 #> k (max): 5 #>  #> ── Levels ── #>  #> ✔ k = 1: 1 factor, 23.2% variance #> ✔ k = 2: 2 factors, 35.5% variance #> ✔ k = 3: 3 factors, 44.6% variance #> ✔ k = 4: 4 factors, 52.2% variance #> ✔ k = 5: 5 factors, 58.4% variance #>  #> ── Edges ── #>  #> 14 of 40 edges have |r| ≥ 0.3 #> ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> Note: This is a series of linked solutions, not a fitted hierarchical model. #> Cross-level edges are descriptive score correlations. Per-level fit indices #> (EFA/ESEM) describe how well a k-factor model fits the items at that level -- #> they do not validate the edges or the hierarchy itself.

title: Bass-Ackwards Hierarchical Structural Analysis
text: autoplot() draws hierarchical diagram. row level (k = 1 top, k = 5 bottom); arrows show narrow factors inherit broad factor. Arrow thickness encodes |r| arrow color encodes direction (blue = positive, red = negative), legend. Primary-parent edges always positive sign alignment, red arrow marks genuine secondary (cross-branch) relationship. Pass direction = "horizontal" left--right layout.  five-factor level cleanly splits Big Five. single broad factor k = 1 (roughly general positive character) differentiates first positive vs. negative affect (k = 2), successively narrower traits.
code: autoplot(x)

title: Bass-ackwards hierarchical structural analysis — ackwards
text: engine = "pca" – original Goldberg (2006) method; fastest; never fails converge; Waller (2007) algebra exact components. rotation = "varimax" – T'=T^-1 property orthogonal rotation enables closed-form W'RW edge algebra keeps within-level factors uncorrelated cross-level edges reflect hierarchical signal. Matches Goldberg (2006), Kim & Eaton (2015), Forbush et al. (2024). Varimax supported rotation; oblique rotation confound -level signal method's core output. cor = "pearson" – silent basis switching. items look ordinal (<= 7 distinct integer values), cli warning suggest cor = "polychoric", available three engines. align_signs = TRUE – unaligned signs make output unreadable. Anchor: m1f1 oriented toward positive manifold; subsequent factor flipped edge primary parent positive. keep_scores = FALSE / keep_fits = FALSE – memory privacy. Scores O(n x Sigmak) often sensitive; raw engine fits can large. recomputable stored r matrix.

text: Score computation. Scores S = Z W / sqrt(score_var), Z = .standardize(data) (item z-scores), W per-level weight matrix stored object, sqrt(score_var) standardizes real score standard deviations (Invariant 1: never assume unit variance). PCA method "components"; EFA/ESEM "tenBerge". Missing data. Score projection applies weights row-wise propagates NAs listwise: observation least one missing item variable produce NA scores every level. differs fitting, uses pairwise-complete correlations. warning issued NA rows detected. Use na.omit(data) scoring NA rows unwanted. Data source. data supplied, scores always recomputed using stored weights – score new observations. data NULL keep_scores = TRUE set fit time, stored scores returned. neither available informative error raised.

title: Compute a layered layout for a bass-ackwards diagram — ba_layout
text: Top-pass – determines left--right order factors level. factor's ordinal rank |r|-weighted mean parents' ranks level , siblings share parent grouped together. Bottom-pass – assigns actual x coordinates. deepest level (level k) spread evenly; every upper-level factor placed simple mean x primary children: factor one primary child lands directly ; factor two primary children lands exactly halfway . Falls back |r|-weighted mean children factors primary children. Spreading resolves remaining overlaps. passes layout shifted single level-1 node always x = 0.

title: Flag redundant or artifactual factors (Forbes 2023 extension) — prune
text: prune() never removes anything ackwards object – annotates factors pruned/prune_reason flags x$prune$nodes (flag-, never removes; object keeps every level). separate, cheap step extraction, can re-prune new thresholds without re-running ackwards(): prune() S3 generic (rather plain function) coexists prune generics already defined recursive-partitioning packages (e.g. rpart::prune) regardless package load order.
code: x <- ackwards(bfi25, k_max = 6, engine = "esem")  # expensive   x |> prune("redundant")                            # cheap, repeatable   x |> prune("redundant", redundancy_r = 0.95)        # no re-extraction

title: Flag redundant or artifactual factors (Forbes 2023 extension) — prune
code: prune(x, ...)  # S3 method for class 'ackwards' prune(   x,   rules = "none",   manual = NULL,   redundancy_r = 0.9,   redundancy_phi = NULL,   min_items = 3L,   orphan_r = 0.5,   ... )

title: Flag redundant or artifactual factors (Forbes 2023 extension) — prune
text: x ackwards object. ... Reserved future methods/arguments. rules Character vector controlling auto-rules run. Default "none" (auto rule; combine manual pure manual pruning, call prune(x) arguments clear existing pruning). Options: "redundant" – identify chains factors connected primary-parent links |r| >= redundancy_r (optionally phi > redundancy_phi). Applies Forbes's (2023) retention rule: keep bottom node chain reaches level k_max (specific); keep top node otherwise. Flagged nodes get pruned = TRUE prune_reason = "redundant" x$prune$nodes. "artifact" (alias "artefact", normalized "artifact") – compute Tucker's congruence coefficient (phi) cross-level factor pairs store x$prune$phi, plus structural signals (few_items/orphan/split_merge) x$prune$structural. factors auto-flagged; artifact identification requires judgment (Forbes, 2023; Wicherts et al., 2016). manual Character vector factor labels (e.g. c("m4f3", "m4f4")) flag directly, addition instead auto rule. Standalone manual pruning supported: prune(x, manual = c("m4f3")). Unknown labels error. node already flagged auto rule keeps prune_reason; otherwise-unflagged manual nodes get prune_reason = "manual". redundancy_r Scalar (0, 1]. Adjacent primary-parent |r| threshold redundancy chains. Default 0.9 (Forbes, 2023). redundancy_phi Scalar (0, 1], NULL (default, auto), NA (explicit opt-). NULL: x$engine == "pca" – phi filter (W'RW algebra exact; phi adds nothing |r| already capture). x$engine "efa" "esem" – automatically set 0.95 (Lorenzo-Seva & ten Berge, 2006). Factor-score indeterminacy -PCA means |r|-alone liberal; conjunctive phi criterion conservative default. cli message announces resolved value. Pass NA disable phi filtering regardless engine. Pass numeric value override engine. min_items Minimum number items factor must primary loader (highest |loading|). Factors fewer min_items primary items flagged few_items = TRUE x$prune$structural. used rules includes "artifact". Default 3L – factor defined one two items -identified frequently extraction artifact rather replicable construct (classic "three-indicator rule"; Forbes, 2023, Fig. 2). orphan_r Threshold orphan structural signal. factor whose maximum adjacent-level |r| (immediately shallower deeper levels) falls orphan_r flagged orphan = TRUE x$prune$structural – connect neighbouring solutions replicate across hierarchy. used rules includes "artifact". Default 0.5 – moderate correlation; factor shares less quarter variance every neighbour structural outlier worth inspecting.

title: Simulated continuous bass-ackwards teaching example (16 items, known hierarchy) — sim16
text: Population model. Sigma = Lambda %*% Phi %*% t(Lambda) + Psi, oblique common-factor model 4 true group factors, sampled via Cholesky factorization (base R; MASS dependency): items load 0.75 true factor (cross-loadings). Factor correlations: 0.45 within metatrait (f1-f2, f3-f4), 0.15 metatraits. Uniquenesses 1 - communality (uniform 0.4375 symmetry design ). Ground-truth hierarchy (verified ackwards(engine = "efa")): k=1 recovers single general factor across 16 items; k=2 splits along metatrait line (i1-i8 vs. i9-i16); k=4 recovers 4 true group factors exactly; suggest_k() reaches 6-criteria consensus k = 4. Idealized design. planted signal strong clean, six suggest_k() criteria converge k = 4 – deliberately easy case, building intuition recovering known hierarchy looks like. Real data rarely agree cleanly: bfi25 criteria span k = 4–6. two datasets complementary teaching foils – sim16 "watch method recover structure planted," bfi25 "reason hierarchy criteria disagree." Present sim16's consensus ideal, norm. Deliberate overextraction artifact k=5. population exactly 4 factors, requesting 5th finds real dimension: EFA produces orphan factor zero primary-loading items. prune(x, "artifact") (default min_items = 3, orphan_r = 0.5), factor flagged few_items orphan. true (non- splitting) factors persist essentially unchanged k=3 onward, parent-child score correlations approach 1 flagged prune(x, "redundant") (|r| >= .9 , default, Tucker's phi >= .95). textbook overextraction artifact, included Forbes/redundancy examples guaranteed finding teach (unlike bfi25, reliably trigger one). regenerate dataset, run source("data-raw/sim16.R") package root (set.seed(42)).

title: Suggest a maximum number of factors for bass-ackwards analysis — suggest_k
text: data data frame numeric matrix (items columns, observations rows). Alternatively, pre-computed correlation matrix may supplied (square, symmetric, numeric matrix unit diagonal). correlation matrix supplied, n_obs required (PA VSS need N), cor argument ignored, Comparison Data (CD) criterion skipped (CD requires raw item distributions resampling). k_max Maximum number factors/components evaluate recommending depth – depth . Defaults min(ncol(data) - 1, 8). Increase expect deeper hierarchy. (Note: distinct meaning ackwards()'s k_max, extraction depth; two share name dial suggest_k() -> ackwards() workflow, just applied different stages.) criteria Character vector criteria compute. subset c("pa_pc", "pa_fa", "map", "vss", "cd"); default five. "pa_pc" "pa_fa" share one parallel-analysis call (neither fast); "map" "vss" share one VSS call. Criteria requested skipped entirely (computation) k_* fields result NA. "vss" selects VSS-1 VSS-2 unit. cor Correlation basis: "pearson" (default) "spearman". match cor argument plan use ackwards(). Ignored data correlation matrix (basis already fixed). n_obs Number observations. Required data pre-computed correlation matrix (PA VSS need N). Ignored raw data supplied (N determined nrow(data)). n_iter Number Monte Carlo iterations parallel analysis. Default 20. Reduce 5 fast/exploratory runs; increase 100+ publication. seed Integer seed passed set.seed() Comparison Data (CD) step. NULL (default) uses current RNG state. Note: parallel-analysis step uses psych::fa.parallel(), respond reliably set.seed() – PA simulation results vary across calls regardless seed. ... Reserved future arguments.

title: Suggest a maximum number of factors for bass-ackwards analysis — suggest_k
text: object class "suggest_k". Print formatted summary; call autoplot() diagnostic scree/criteria plot. list contains: k_parallel_pc Recommended k PC-based parallel analysis (NA_integer_ "pa_pc" criteria). k_parallel_fa Recommended k FA-based parallel analysis (NA_integer_ FA factor exceeded random threshold, "pa_fa" criteria). k_map Recommended k MAP (NA_integer_ "map" criteria). k_vss1 Recommended k VSS complexity-1 (NA_integer_ "vss" criteria). k_vss2 Recommended k VSS complexity-2 (NA_integer_ "vss" criteria). k_cd Recommended k Comparison Data (NA_integer_ "cd" criteria, EFAtools installed, CD fails). cd_available Logical; TRUE "cd" requested, EFAtools found, CD ran successfully. criteria Data frame one row per k: k, ev_obs (observed PC eigenvalue), ev_obs_fa (observed FA eigenvalue), pa_pc_quant / pa_fa_quant (95th-pct simulated eigenvalue basis), pa_pc_suggested / pa_fa_suggested (logical retention), map, vss1, vss2. Columns non-requested criteria NA. criteria_requested Character vector criteria requested (therefore computed). k_max, n_obs, n_vars, cor Metadata.

title: Suggest a maximum number of factors for bass-ackwards analysis — suggest_k
text: Criteria available (controlled criteria argument): PA-PC ("pa_pc") – Horn (1965) parallel analysis PC eigenvalues. Compares observed eigenvalues random correlation matrices; suggests retaining components whose eigenvalues exceed 95th percentile chance. Tends overextract; treat upper bound. PA-FA ("pa_fa") – Horn (1965) parallel analysis using common-factor eigenvalues. conservative PA-PC better match EFA ESEM engines ackwards(). Shares one psych::fa.parallel() call PA-PC. MAP ("map") – Velicer (1976) Minimum Average Partial criterion. Finds k minimises average squared partial correlation remaining extracting k components. Usually conservative. Shares one psych::vss() call VSS. VSS ("vss") – Revelle & Rocklin (1979) Simple Structure fit complexities 1 2 (VSS-1 VSS-2). Finds k maximising fit simple loading structure. Shares one psych::vss() call MAP. CD ("cd") – Ruscio & Roche (2012) Comparison Data. Resamples observed item distributions generate comparison eigenvalue profiles; retains factors adding one longer improves RMSE beyond chance. Requires EFAtools package (install separately). Skipped informational message EFAtools absent correlation matrix supplied. PA (bases), MAP, VSS share correlation matrix ackwards(). CD operates raw data matrix directly (required resampling).

title: Tidy an ackwards object into a long data frame — tidy.ackwards
text: x ackwards object. extract: "edges" (default) – one row per directed -level edge: , , level_from, level_to, r, is_primary, above_cut. "loadings" – one row per item x factor x level: level, factor, item, loading, se, ci_lower, ci_upper. se, ci_lower, ci_upper populated engine = "esem" (produces rotation-aware loading SEs); NA PCA EFA. confidence level controlled conf_level. "variance" – one row per factor x level: level, factor, proportion, cumulative. proportions total item variance 0-1 scale (multiply 100 percentage). "fit" – one row per fit statistic x level: level, statistic, value. PCA objects statistics eigenvalues; EFA objects chi, dof, p_value, RMSEA, TLI, BIC; ESEM chi, dof, p_value, CFI, TLI, RMSEA, SRMR, BIC. ESEM scaled-test estimator ("WLSMV"/"ULSMV" ordinal data, "MLR" continuous), whole row – chi/dof/ p_value CFI/TLI/RMSEA – reports lavaan's mean--variance-adjusted ("scaled") variant, every quantity shares one scaling. matters WLSMV/ULSMV: naive chi-square valid reference distribution (lavaan's summary() labels p-value "Unknown"), naive CFI/TLI badly optimistic ordinal data (Xia & Yang, 2019). "ML" scaled variant, reports naive values (correct ones ML). SRMR scaled variant reported -. BIC NA WLSMV/ULSMV (proper log-likelihood limited-information estimator) populated ML/MLR. Use format = "wide" one row per non-anchor level (k >= 2; saturated 1-factor anchor dropped, matching summary() autoplot(= "fit")), one column per statistic. Conventional fit cutoffs (Hu & Bentler 1999) shown reference lines autoplot(= "fit") inline summary(), returned pass/fail column – contested thresholds, report-, never gate anything (see functions' docs). format oriented EFA/ESEM model-fit statistics; PCA "statistics" per-component eigenvalues. "nodes" – Forbes-extension pruning annotations (requires prune != "none" object created). One row per factor across levels: id, level, pruned, prune_reason. Returns empty data frame columns pruning applied. "scores" – long-format per-observation factor scores (requires keep_scores = TRUE fit time use augment.ackwards() --fly computation). Columns: obs (row index), level, factor, score. primary_only = "edges" . TRUE, returns just factor's primary-parent edge (is_primary == TRUE) – lineage tree diagram draws solid arrows. Default FALSE (edges). Errors value . sort = "edges" . One "none" (default, natural order) "strength" (descending |r|). Ignored values . format = "fit" . One "long" (default, one row per statistic x level) "wide" (one row per level, one column per statistic). Errors values . conf_level = "loadings" . Confidence level loading intervals; default 0.95. intervals computed loading ± qnorm((1 + conf_level) / 2) * se NA engines carry SEs (PCA, EFA). Errors values . ... Ignored.

ackwards 0.1.0
text: First public release. Licensed MIT.

title: ackwards 0.1.0
text: Three engines available via engine argument ackwards(): PCA (engine = "pca") — original Goldberg (2006) bass-ackwards method. Fastest; never fails converge; exact W′RW algebra -level edges. EFA (engine = "efa") — exploratory factor analysis via psych::fa() tenBerge scoring weights closed-form algebra path. ESEM (engine = "esem") — EFA estimated SEM framework via lavaan::efa() WLSMV ordinal estimation, per-level fit indices (CFI, TLI, RMSEA, SRMR), rotation-aware standard errors. Required clinical/HiTOP workflow. engines use varimax (orthogonal) rotation — rotation compatible W′RW algebra. Oblique rotation unsupported design.

title: ackwards 0.1.0
text: suggest_k() runs five complementary retention criteria returns consensus range: PA-PC PA-FA — parallel analysis PC FA eigenvalue bases (psych::fa.parallel(fa = "")). MAP (Velicer 1976) VSS-1/VSS-2 (Revelle & Rocklin 1979) via psych::vss(). Comparison Data (Ruscio & Roche 2012) via EFAtools::CD() — skipped gracefully EFAtools installed. autoplot.suggest_k() produces ggplot2 diagnostic: four-panel 2×2 grid (scree/PA, MAP, VSS, CD RMSE) EFAtools installed, three-panel single-column layout (scree/PA, MAP, VSS) otherwise. returned object also carries cd_rmse (column means CD’s RMSE eigenvalue matrix) use CD panel.

title: ackwards 0.1.0
text: -level factor-score correlations computed via closed-form W′RW algebra linear engines (PCA, EFA, ESEM tenBerge weights) — scores need materialized. Standardization uses real score SDs sqrt(diag(W′RW)) rather assuming unit variance. materialized-scores fallback available cross-check test verifies two routes agree within tolerance.

title: ackwards 0.1.0
text: Pass cor = "polychoric" use polychoric correlations basis PCA EFA. ESEM, lavaan’s polychoric matrix used edge computation (consistent fitted model). WLSMV estimator default cor = "polychoric" ESEM. ackwards() detects likely-ordinal columns (≤ 7 distinct integer values) emits suppressible warning cor = "pearson" set.

title: ackwards 0.1.0
text: Three strategies via missing ackwards(): "pairwise" (default) — pairwise-complete correlations; warns NAs present. "listwise" — complete cases , consistent N across fit edges. "fiml" — Full Information ML via lavaan (ESEM ML/MLR ); edges derived FIML saturated model.

title: ackwards 0.1.0
text: pairs = "" computes -level correlations every level pair, revealing skip-level associations. prune = "redundant" flags chains adjacent |r| ≥ 0.9 (Forbes retention rule). prune = "artefact" computes Tucker’s φ researcher inspection; auto-flagging. autoplot() renders pruned views (drop_pruned = TRUE) skip-level arcs.

title: ackwards 0.1.0
text: ggplot2 layered diagram extensive customization: edge color sign, width/alpha |r|, solid/dashed strength; monochrome mode (mono); APA-formatted correlation labels (show_r, r_digits, r_label_size); custom node labels (node_labels); primary-mode (primary_only); level axis labels (show_level_labels); arrowhead toggle (show_arrows); fixed edge width (edge_linewidth); legend toggle (legend); Forbes-style pruned view (drop_pruned, compress_levels).

title: ackwards 0.1.0
text: top_items(x, level, cut, n, sort) — lists salient items factor, filtered |loading| >= cut (default 0.3) sorted descending absolute loading. Arguments level, n, sort allow subsetting levels, capping items per factor, controlling order. Returns top_items S3 object grouped cli print method. label_template(x, style) — generates named-character-vector scaffold autoplot(x, node_labels = ...). Styles: "id" (default), "forbes" (level-letter + within-level index: "A1", "B1", "B2", …), "blank". Factor IDs canonical layout order; editable c(...) literal also printed copy-paste. See vignette("ackwards-interpret") end--end naming workflow: reading factor top_items(), hierarchy-aware naming (parent vs. child, blends), sign-alignment caveat, top_items() → label_template() → autoplot(node_labels = ...) round-trip.

title: ackwards 0.1.0
text: psych moved Imports — default PCA EFA engines require psych; placing Suggests meant mandatory install prompt core functionality. SEM (lavaan) plotting (ggplot2) stacks remain Suggests. GPArotation removed — varimax rotation routes base stats::varimax; GPArotation never loaded supported path. bfi25 example dataset bundled — 1 000-row, 25-item subset SAPA/IPIP Big Five data (sampled psych::bfi). Used throughout examples vignettes run without reaching psych’s namespace. See ?bfi25 provenance @source.

title: ackwards 0.1.0
text: ackwards() suggest_k() now accept pre-computed correlation matrix place raw item data, detected automatically matrix shape (square, symmetric, unit diagonal). Engine gating — "pca" "efa" supported; "esem" errors clearly (lavaan requires raw data WLSMV estimation per-level fit indices). n_obs argument — new optional argument functions. Required engine = "efa" matrix (psych needs N chi-square/RMSEA/TLI); optional engine = "pca" (stored NA omitted). Ignored (warning) raw data supplied. Edge correctness — edges ackwards(R, ...) match ackwards(data, ...) exactly correlation matrix: routes use closed-form W'RW algebra. cor missing arguments — ignored (warning set) matrix input. $cor stored NA_character_ printed "(user-supplied matrix)". Score paths blocked — keep_scores = TRUE, augment(), tidy(= "scores") error clearly: individual-level scoring requires row-level item responses. CD skipped suggest_k() — Comparison Data resamples raw item distributions skipped info note matrix supplied. Validation — square, numeric, finite, symmetric, unit diagonal, |r| <= 1, NA; synthesises V1..Vp dimnames absent; warns (auto-smooth) positive-definite; errors clear message covariance matrix detected.

title: ackwards 0.1.0
text: print.ackwards() — compact cli summary card. summary.ackwards() — per-level variance, fit indices, lineage list, pruning notes. tidy(x, = "edges" / "loadings" / "loadings_se" / "variance" / "nodes" / "scores") — long-format tidy tibbles. = "edges", primary_only = TRUE returns just factor’s primary-parent edge (lineage tree) sort = "strength" orders |r|. = "loadings_se" returns rotation-aware loading standard errors (ESEM ). glance(x) — one-row model-level summary. augment(x, data = ...) — appends factor scores data frame; recomputes stored weights scores materialized fit time.

title: ackwards 0.1.0
text: Vignette comparison tables reworked legibility: ackwards-engines ackwards-ordinal — stacked long-format kable tables replaced wide gt tables: one row per item/edge, one column per engine/basis, plus explicit Δ column teaching point (EFA/polychoric attenuation). Factor sign correspondence asserted differencing; edge tables surface primary-parent disagreements NA rather hiding . ackwards-forbes — prune-nodes raw tidy() print replaced styled gt table; narrated counts (n_adj, n_all, n_redundant, top skip-edge) now computed via inline R expressions can never go stale. gt added Suggests (vignette table formatting ; never loaded core).

title: ackwards 0.1.0
text: Test suite reaches 100% line coverage. Genuinely unreachable defensive branches (engine fallbacks, lavaan error/warning handlers) excluded via # nocov rather brittle environment-dependent tests.
